title: Reframe dashboard status

tasks.title: Implement ADV-centered latest-source reducer
tasks.verification: RED tr_mqv6wl1a_a6dbf979 failed because old event-centered lanes lacked latest-status keys and suppression. GREEN tr_mqv74wne_95632b32 passed `bun test bin/lib/dashboard/attention.test.ts` (6 tests, 19 expects).
tasks.implementation_summary: Replaced source-event primary lanes with ADV-centered latest-status lanes: `needs_attention`, `running`, `ready_landed`, `backlog`, and secondary `unmatched_source`. Added `adv_change_status` lane item model and latest-source reducer. Workflow latest identity is `(workflow_id ?? name/title) + branch`; recency uses max valid `run_started_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`; deployment identity is environment/title + ref. Same-identity newer success/running suppresses older failures; distinct workflows/branches remain visible; missing timestamps cannot suppress valid failures; degraded sources stay visible without false green.

sources.label: GitHub REST: List workflow runs for a repository (object schema)
sources.summary: Workflow Run object exposes stable identity + recency fields: name (string|null), workflow_id (int, stable), head_branch (string|null), run_number, run_attempt, run_started_at, created_at, updated_at, conclusion, status, previous_attempt_url. actions/runs returns most-recent-first and supports ?branch= and ?head_sha= server-side narrowing. Confirms design can derive a deterministic 'latest same-identity' fold.

sources.label: GitHub REST: Re-run a workflow / rerun-failed-jobs
sources.summary: POST /runs/{run_id}/rerun and /rerun-failed-jobs re-run using the SAME run_id (increments run_attempt, sets previous_attempt_url) — a rerun does NOT mint a new run object. Therefore stale-failure noise comes from DISTINCT runs (new push/event = new id) of the same workflow+branch, not from duplicate ids. The fold key must be workflow identity + branch, and recency must use run_started_at/created_at, not run id.

sources.label: Local: github.ts fetch shape
sources.summary: Current client fetches actions/runs?per_page=100 unfiltered (all historical runs across all workflows/branches) and only the latest deployment status per deployment id. This is the raw event firehose the new fold must reduce.

sources.label: Local: attention.ts current (broken) suppression
sources.summary: isSuccessfulHistory only folds success/skipped runs into summaries; it NEVER lets a newer same-identity success/running suppress an older FAILURE. Failures unconditionally attention.unshift (line 109/117). This is the exact root cause of stale CI/deploy failures persisting after a corrected rerun.

sources.label: Local: correlation.ts structural-only linking
sources.summary: Linking is purely structural (branch/sha/ops-key index, single-match only, ambiguous>1 => unlinked). Design's 'source linking remains structural only' is already satisfied and should be preserved untouched.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The new design is the canonical fix and the boring one. Reducing an append-only source-event stream (workflow_runs, deployment_statuses) to a single derived latest-state per stable identity is the textbook read-model/projection fold (CQRS read model / event-to-state reduce). The existing code already half-implements this fold for SUCCESS history but omits the failure case, which is precisely the user-observed bug. Making ADV changes the primary cards and deriving latest linked source state per change is a clean inversion that matches the structural-link index already present. Key correctness invariants from authoritative docs: (1) Identity must be workflow_id (or name as fallback) + head_branch for runs, and environment + ref for deployments — NOT run id, because rerun reuses the id while distinct push runs create new ids. (2) Recency ordering must use run_started_at || created_at || updated_at (timestamps), never id ordering or array order, since attention.ts:377-383 already shows id-agnostic timestamp comparison is the established pattern. (3) Suppression must be scoped to the SAME identity tuple only — design's 'unrelated workflows/branches do not suppress' is correct and prevents a green deploy from masking a red lint. (4) Degraded reads stay visible / no false green is already enforced via degradedSources threading (server.ts:104-140) and must remain a hard rule: a missing/timed-out source must render as degraded, never collapse to success.

validation.notes: VALIDATED. Design direction is architecturally sound and is the simplest correct model. Proceed, but pin the identity/recency/suppression-scope invariants below as tests — they are the failure-prone seams.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED — implement the per-identity latest-state fold. Concrete adjustments: (1) Run identity key = `${workflow_id ?? name ?? 'unknown'}\u0000${head_branch ?? ''}`; deployment identity key = `${environment}\u0000${ref}`. Do NOT key on run id. (2) Recency = max(run_started_at, created_at, updated_at) parsed as timestamp; reuse the existing isIsoLikeTimestamp/latestItem comparator (attention.ts:377-383) rather than inventing a new one (LBP). (3) Fold rule: within an identity group, the newest event wins; a newer success/in_progress/queued REPLACES (not merely summarizes) an older failure/cancelled/timed_out. Only emit needs_attention when the WINNER of the group is a failure-class status. (4) Suppression must never cross identity tuples — assert this with a test: two workflows on same branch, one red one green, both surface independently. (5) Keep correlation.ts structural-only; build the fold as a new pure reduce step between correlate and lane-bucketing so it is independently testable. (6) New lane keys (needs_attention/running/ready_landed/backlog + secondary unmatched_source) require updating the frozen `Object.keys(lanes)` assertion in attention.test.ts:82-87 and the ui.ts lane-template (ui.ts:75-80) data-lane attributes — these are coupled contracts that will silently break rendering if not co-updated. (7) Preserve degraded-source visibility: a degraded/timeout/missing GitHub read must keep the change card's source state as unknown/degraded, never default to ready_landed/green (guard with a test feeding empty github data + a degraded source).
follow_ups: Consider adding ?branch= server-side narrowing to actions/runs fetch (github.ts:150) to shrink the firehose, but only as an optimization AFTER the client-side fold is correct — server filtering alone does not solve cross-run staleness.
Add explicit test: rerun scenario where same run id changes conclusion failure->success across two polls must show ready/running, not attention.
Add explicit test: distinct run ids (new push) same workflow+branch, newest=success suppresses older=failure; AND newest=failure surfaces despite older=success.

wisdom.content: For CI dashboards, derive status via event-to-state fold before lane bucketing. Use stable source identity (`workflow_id ?? name` + branch) plus timestamp recency; never use run id for supersession because reruns/new pushes have different semantics. Pick representative source by overall state so attention cards show the failing current check, not merely newest green check.

For dashboard refactors that intentionally change lane keys, updating model/server/UI tests in sequence kept the blast radius controlled: first prove pure reducer semantics, then server API shape, then static UI contracts, then targeted/full bin verification.

proposal.approval_evidence: Proposal approved by explicit user reply `approve` after investigation confirmed current dashboard is still source-event/history centered and can show stale failed CI/deployment events after reruns. Direction: ADV-change-centered status board with latest source state and links to CI/deployment details.

Contract minted with 27 items.

design.approval_evidence: Design completed and independently validated. Validator returned VALIDATED. Key decisions: implement pure latest-state reduce before lane bucketing; workflow run identity is `(workflow_id ?? name) + branch`, never run id; recency uses max(run_started_at, created_at, updated_at); suppression scoped to same identity only; lane keys/UI must be co-updated; degraded reads never default green. No product tradeoff, conflict, or contract-compromise risk remains.

items.text: Primary visible cards are ADV changes, not raw `workflow_run`/`deployment`/`pull` event cards.

Older failed runs no longer create false attention after a newer same workflow+branch run succeeds.

Unmatched source work remains inspectable, but secondary.
Given multiple workflow runs exist for the same workflow and branch, when a newer run succeeds, then older failed runs do not place the change in `Needs attention`.
Given multiple workflow runs exist for the same workflow and branch, when the newest run is failing, then the linked change appears in `Needs attention` with a link to that latest failing run.
Given the newest workflow run for a linked change is queued, pending, in_progress, or running, when the dashboard renders, then the change appears in `Running`.
Given the newest workflow/deployment state for a linked change is success or landed, when the dashboard renders, then the change appears in `Ready / landed`.
Given an ADV change has no correlated source activity and is draft/non-active, when the dashboard renders, then it appears in `Backlog / inventory`, not `Needs attention`.

Given source links/metadata render on change-centered cards, when the dashboard loads, then safe URL allowlisting, HTML escaping, no-secret behavior, and no mutation controls remain intact.

documents.proposal:
# Reframe dashboard status

## Problem
The dashboard still surfaces stale CI/deployment failures that were later corrected by reruns. It groups historical events, but does not reduce each branch/ADV change to the latest meaningful source state.

Live evidence after `simplifyDashboardView`:
- `pokeedge` still shows attention failures such as old deployment/workflow failures from 2026-06-22/25.
- `attention` groups are built from raw historical workflow_run/deployment items rather than latest-per-branch/workflow status.
- GitHub fetch reads recent workflow runs/deployments; lane builder classifies all failures before supersession/latest-state reduction.
- Current board is source-event centered (`workflow_run`, `deployment`, `pull`) rather than ADV-change centered.

User feedback: “failures showing for ci runs that were corrected and re-run” and “rewrite to be centered around adv changes and then have links to their ci runs. columns reflect where their latest ci run is or landed.”

## Scope
Affected surface:
- `bin/lib/dashboard/correlation.ts` — source-to-ADV matching/link data.
- `bin/lib/dashboard/attention.ts` or successor lane model — derived dashboard lanes/cards.
- `bin/lib/dashboard/server.ts` — dashboard state assembly.
- `bin/lib/dashboard/ui.ts` — rendered columns/cards.
- `bin/lib/dashboard/*.test.ts` — latest-status and read-only coverage.

Auth model remains unchanged:
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` first, then bounded `gh auth token` fallback.
- No browser login flow.
- No token display.
- Auth degraded state remains a separate setup/degraded card.

Error/fallback behavior:
- If GitHub/ADV reads fail, show degraded source cards as today.
- If a source item cannot be linked structurally, keep it in secondary unmatched source section.
- If latest-state derivation lacks enough data, fall back to conservative visible unknown/unmatched status, not false green.

## Value target
Dashboard should be an ADV work/status board:
- Primary cards are ADV changes.
- Each ADV card shows latest linked CI/deployment/source state.
- Columns represent current state of the change, not historical event categories.
- Historical runs are available as links/details, but never drive status if superseded.

## Initial direction
Replace raw source-event-first lanes with change-centered derived rows:
1. Correlate GitHub PRs/workflow runs/deployments to ADV changes by branch/SHA as today.
2. For each ADV change, derive latest source state by workflow/deployment identity.
3. Ignore older failures when a newer run for the same workflow + branch/head status succeeds or is running.
4. Render columns by latest state:
   - Needs attention: latest CI/deploy failing or auth/data degradation.
   - Running: latest CI/deploy queued/in_progress/running.
   - Ready / landed: latest CI/deploy successful, PR merged/closed, or deployment landed.
   - Backlog/inventory: draft/non-active ADV changes with no current source activity.
5. Show source links/details inside each ADV card: PR, latest CI runs, latest deployment, recent history.
6. Keep unmatched source work as secondary section, not primary columns.

## Acceptance criteria draft
1. Given multiple workflow runs exist for the same workflow and branch, when a newer run succeeds, then older failed runs do not place the change in Needs attention.
2. Given an ADV change has correlated workflow/deployment activity, when the dashboard renders, then the ADV change card shows latest source status and links to latest runs/deployments.
3. Given a source item cannot be linked to any ADV change, when the dashboard renders, then it appears in a secondary unmatched section, not the primary change columns.
4. Given an ADV change is draft/non-active with no latest source activity, when the dashboard renders, then it appears in backlog/inventory, not attention.
5. Given active/running CI exists for a change, when the dashboard renders, then the change appears in Running based on latest run status.
6. Given latest CI/deployment status is success/landed, when the dashboard renders, then the change appears in Ready/Landed rather than attention.
7. Given source cards include URLs/metadata, when rendering new change-centered cards, safe URL allowlisting, HTML escaping, no-secret behavior, and no mutation controls remain intact.
8. Given dashboard tests run, when `bun test bin/` completes, then latest-run precedence, change-centered columns, unmatched secondary section, and read-only constraints are covered by tests.
documents.problemStatement: Dashboard still reports stale CI/deployment failures because it is source-event/history centered. The desired value is an ADV-centered board where each change shows its latest CI/deploy state, with source links as supporting details.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement: Reframe dashboard status

## Problem
The dashboard still reports stale CI/deployment failures because it is source-event/history centered. Grouping reduced volume, but older failed runs/deployments still influence `Attention` even when newer source activity for the same branch/workflow/deployment supersedes them.

## Objectives
1. Make the primary dashboard an ADV-change-centered status board.
2. Derive each change’s current status from latest linked source state, not raw historical events.
3. Suppress stale failed workflow/deployment events when a newer same-identity source event supersedes them.
4. Keep links/details to supporting CI runs, PRs, and deployments on the ADV change card.
5. Move unmatched source work into a secondary section.
6. Preserve local-only, read-only, sanitized dashboard behavior.
7. Preserve current GitHub auth model and degraded-source handling.

## Success criteria
- Primary visible cards are ADV changes, not raw `workflow_run`/`deployment`/`pull` event cards.
- A change’s column reflects latest linked CI/deployment status.
- Older failed runs no longer create false attention after a newer same workflow+branch run succeeds.
- Current failures and running work remain visible.
- Unmatched source work remains inspectable, but secondary.

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given multiple workflow runs exist for the same workflow and branch, when a newer run succeeds, then older failed runs do not place the change in `Needs attention`.
2. Given multiple workflow runs exist for the same workflow and branch, when the newest run is failing, then the linked change appears in `Needs attention` with a link to that latest failing run.
3. Given the newest workflow run for a linked change is queued, pending, in_progress, or running, when the dashboard renders, then the change appears in `Running`.
4. Given the newest workflow/deployment state for a linked change is success or landed, when the dashboard renders, then the change appears in `Ready / landed`.
5. Given an ADV change has no correlated source activity and is draft/non-active, when the dashboard renders, then it appears in `Backlog / inventory`, not `Needs attention`.
6. Given an ADV change has correlated source activity, when the dashboard renders, then the change card shows the change title/id/gate plus latest PR, CI, and deployment links/details where present.
7. Given a source item cannot be linked to any ADV change, when the dashboard renders, then it appears in a secondary unmatched source section and does not determine primary columns.
8. Given GitHub or ADV reads fail, when the dashboard renders, then degraded source cards remain visible and no false green/latest status is inferred.
9. Given source links/metadata render on change-centered cards, when the dashboard loads, then safe URL allowlisting, HTML escaping, no-secret behavior, and no mutation controls remain intact.
10. Given dashboard tests run, when `bun test bin/` completes, then latest-run precedence, change-centered columns, unmatched secondary section, degraded fallback, and read-only constraints are covered by tests.

## Constraints
- Keep dashboard local-only at `127.0.0.1`.
- Read-only UI only: no rerun/merge/approve/deploy/archive/cancel controls.
- No token/secret display.
- GitHub auth model unchanged: `GITHUB_TOKEN` first, then bounded `gh auth token` fallback.
- No fuzzy title-based correctness; source-to-change linking must remain structural (branch/SHA/known keys).
- If latest derivation cannot prove a superseding newer source state, preserve visibility as unknown/unmatched/attention as appropriate rather than hiding risk.

## Avoidances
- No GitHub mutation controls.
- No ADV mutation controls.
- No server-side preference store.
- No hosted dashboard behavior.
- No false green from missing/ambiguous data.
- No primary raw event wall.
documents.design:
# Design: Reframe dashboard status

## Summary
Replace the current source-event lane model with an ADV-change-centered status model.

Current architecture:
- `github.ts` reads open PRs, recent workflow runs, deployments, and deployment statuses.
- `correlation.ts` matches raw source events to changes by branch/SHA/ops keys.
- `attention.ts` projects and groups source events into lanes.
- `ui.ts` renders lane items and grouped details.

Root cause validated:
- `github.ts` fetches recent Actions runs broadly.
- `correlation.ts` links raw events structurally.
- `attention.ts` folds successes into summaries but sends failures directly to attention with no newer-success/newer-running suppression.
- Therefore old failed workflow/deployment events can remain attention items after newer same-identity reruns.

## New model
Introduce a pure event-to-state reduce step between correlation and lane bucketing. Keep dashboard API read-only and sanitized.

### Lane keys and labels
Use new primary lane keys:
- `needs_attention` → `Needs attention`
- `running` → `Running`
- `ready_landed` → `Ready / landed`
- `backlog` → `Backlog / inventory`

Add a secondary unmatched section/lane:
- `unmatched_source` → `Unmatched source`

Compatibility note: UI is known local consumer. Tests must pin new keys and labels. Existing tests that assert `attention`, `active`, `unmatched`, `inventory` must be deliberately updated.

### Primary item type
Add a typed ADV-centered dashboard item, e.g.:

```ts
export interface ChangeStatusLaneItem {
  kind: "adv_change_status";
  changeId: string;
  title: string;
  status: string;
  gate: string;
  progress: string;
  latest: {
    overall: "attention" | "running" | "ready_landed" | "backlog" | "unknown";
    pr?: SourceStatusSummary;
    ci?: SourceStatusSummary;
    deployment?: SourceStatusSummary;
  };
  sources: {
    prs: ProjectedSourceLaneItem[];
    workflow_runs: ProjectedSourceLaneItem[];
    deployments: ProjectedSourceLaneItem[];
  };
  metadata?: LaneCardMetadata[];
}
```

`SourceStatusSummary`:
- `kind`: `pull` | `workflow_run` | `deployment`
- `title`
- `status`
- `url?`
- `updated_at?`
- `metadata?`

## Data flow
1. Correlate source items structurally as today.
2. Project linked/unlinked source items into typed source summaries as today.
3. Build a map `changeId -> ChangeStatusAccumulator`.
4. Seed every ADV change into the map.
5. Attach linked PR/workflow/deployment source items to the matching change.
6. Reduce each change’s sources to latest status summaries.
7. Classify the change into one primary lane.
8. Keep unlinked source items in secondary `unmatched_source`.

## Latest-state rules
### Workflow runs
Identity key:
`(workflow_id ?? workflow name/title) + head_branch`

Do not key on `run_id`: GitHub reruns reuse the same run id with increasing `run_attempt`, while new pushes create distinct run ids. Correct stale-failure suppression requires workflow identity + branch and timestamp recency, not run id dedupe.

Recency timestamp:
`max(run_started_at, created_at, updated_at)` among valid ISO-like strings.

Supersession:
- Newer `success`/`skipped` for same workflow identity + branch suppresses older `failure` for that same key.
- Newer `queued`/`pending`/`in_progress`/`running` suppresses older failure for that same key and classifies as running.
- Distinct workflow names/ids or branches do not suppress each other.
- Missing/invalid timestamps cannot suppress a valid-timestamp failure; preserve conservative visibility.

### Deployments
Identity key:
`environment/title + ref`

Use deployment status `updated_at` when present, otherwise deployment `updated_at`, otherwise created time.

Latest deployment state determines deployment summary for that key. A newer success/inactive/success-like landed state can supersede older failure only for the same environment+ref.

### PRs
Open PRs linked to an ADV change appear on the change card.
PR state alone should not create failure unless GitHub explicitly reports an error status through workflow/deployment data. Current GitHub fetch only asks open PRs, so current implementation must not infer merged from absence.

## Overall lane classification
For each change:
1. If any latest linked workflow/deployment summary is attention status (`failure`, `failed`, `error`, `cancelled`, `timed_out`, `degraded`) → `needs_attention`.
2. Else if any latest linked workflow/deployment summary is running status (`queued`, `requested`, `waiting`, `pending`, `in_progress`, `running`) → `running`.
3. Else if any latest linked workflow/deployment summary is success/landed (`success`, `skipped`, `inactive`, deployment success-like state) → `ready_landed`.
4. Else → `backlog`.

Do not infer green from missing GitHub data. Degraded source cards remain visible in `needs_attention` as separate `degraded_source` items. If GitHub read fails, do not infer source status for changes.

## Unmatched source section
`unmatched_source` contains source items that do not structurally match any ADV change. It is secondary and compact:
- open PRs first
- latest unsuperseded failures next
- historical successful/deployment history grouped/summarized

Unmatched source items do not determine primary change columns.

## UI rendering
Update `ui.ts` to render:
- project stats for new lane labels
- `adv_change_status` cards as primary cards
- source summaries/links inside each card
- recent history in read-only `<details>`
- secondary unmatched source lane/section

Keep:
- `safeUrl`
- `escapeHtml`
- no forms/mutation controls
- no links inside `<summary>` if summaries are used

## Safety and fallback
- Source-to-change linking remains structural only: branch/SHA/known correlation keys. No fuzzy title matching for correctness.
- If timestamps are missing or ambiguous, do not hide older failures based on unproven supersession.
- If GitHub/ADV reads fail, show degraded source cards and avoid false green.
- If source shape changes, projection should fall back to unknown/unmatched display rather than throwing or hiding.

## Test strategy
Add/update Bun tests:
- Lane model tests:
  - newer same workflow+branch success suppresses older failure.
  - newest same workflow+branch failure produces `needs_attention`.
  - newest running state produces `running`.
  - distinct branch/workflow failures are not suppressed by unrelated successes.
  - missing timestamp does not suppress valid failure.
  - draft/no-source change goes to `backlog`.
  - unlinked source remains in `unmatched_source`.
- Server tests:
  - end-to-end state shape uses new lane keys and ADV-centered items.
  - degraded GitHub read keeps degraded card and does not create false ready status.
- UI tests:
  - new labels and `data-lane` attrs render.
  - change-centered card structure renders source links/details.
  - source links/details render through safe URL and escaping.
  - no mutation controls/forms.
- Full verification: `bun test bin/`.

## Validator outcome
Independent validator verdict: `VALIDATED`.

Adjustments applied:
- Fold key for workflow runs uses `workflow_id ?? name` + branch, never run id.
- Recency uses max of `run_started_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`.
- Suppression is same-identity only; unrelated workflow/branch failures stay visible.
- Lane-key/UI coupling must be co-updated and pinned by tests.
- Degraded reads must never default to green.
- Implement fold as pure reduce step before lane bucketing.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome

Reframed the local dashboard from a source-event/history board into an ADV-change-centered latest-status board. The dashboard now derives each change’s current state from the latest structurally linked CI/deployment events, so older failed runs are not shown as current failures after a newer same workflow+branch run succeeds or is running.

## Verdict

APPROVED

## What Was Built

1. Added `adv_change_status` primary dashboard cards.
2. Replaced primary lane keys with latest-status lanes: `needs_attention`, `running`, `ready_landed`, `backlog`, and secondary `unmatched_source`.
3. Added a pure latest-source reducer before lane bucketing.
4. Workflow run identity uses `(workflow_id ?? name/title) + branch`; never run id.
5. Recency uses max valid `run_started_at`, `created_at`, and `updated_at` timestamps.
6. Newer same-identity success/running suppresses older failures.
7. Distinct workflow/branch failures remain visible.
8. Missing/invalid timestamps do not suppress valid failures.
9. ADV cards show latest PR, CI, deployment summaries plus read-only source details.
10. Unmatched source work remains secondary.
11. Safe URL allowlisting, HTML escaping, no-secret behavior, degraded-source fallback, and no mutation controls were preserved.

## What Was Verified

- Contract matrix: 27/27 rows pass/respected.
- Acceptance reviewer: `reframeDashboardStatus|change:review:acceptance|adv-reviewer|1` returned READY.
- Targeted dashboard tests: `tr_mqv7ff29_5df2600b` passed 20 tests, 119 assertions.
- Full bin suite: `tr_mqv7g1mj_6c94a733` passed `bun test bin/` with 165 tests, 491 assertions.

## Remaining Concerns

None for the accepted contract. The installed dashboard service still runs the previous release until this change is archived/merged and the service is restarted from trunk.
documents.acceptance:
# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-26T17:35:37.210Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | Primary visible cards are ADV changes, not raw `workflow_run`/`deployment`/`pull` event cards. | pass | Primary visible cards now use `adv_change_status` model; tests tr_mqv74wne_95632b32 and tr_mqv7ff29_5df2600b passed. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | A change’s column reflects latest linked CI/deployment status. | pass | Lane classification derives from latest linked CI/deployment state; attention/server tests verify needs_attention, running, ready_landed, backlog. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | Older failed runs no longer create false attention after a newer same workflow+branch run succeeds. | pass | Test `centers lanes on ADV changes and suppresses stale same-identity CI failures` proves newer same workflow+branch success suppresses older failure; tr_mqv74wne_95632b32 passed. |
| SC4 | success_criterion | Current failures and running work remain visible. | pass | Newest failing/running sources remain visible in needs_attention/running; tests tr_mqv74wne_95632b32 passed. |
| SC5 | success_criterion | Unmatched source work remains inspectable, but secondary. | pass | Unlinked source appears in secondary `unmatched_source`; tests tr_mqv74wne_95632b32 and tr_mqv79h3f_d3ed3f73 passed. |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | Given multiple workflow runs exist for the same workflow and branch, when a newer run succeeds, then older failed runs do not place the change in `Needs attention`. | pass | Newer same workflow+branch success suppresses older failed run in tr_mqv74wne_95632b32. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | Given multiple workflow runs exist for the same workflow and branch, when the newest run is failing, then the linked change appears in `Needs attention` with a link to that latest failing run. | pass | Newest same workflow+branch failure yields `needs_attention` and latest failing CI summary; tr_mqv74wne_95632b32 passed. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | Given the newest workflow run for a linked change is queued, pending, in_progress, or running, when the dashboard renders, then the change appears in `Running`. | pass | Newest in_progress run yields `running`; tr_mqv74wne_95632b32 passed. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | Given the newest workflow/deployment state for a linked change is success or landed, when the dashboard renders, then the change appears in `Ready / landed`. | pass | Latest deployment inactive/success-like state yields `ready_landed`; tr_mqv74wne_95632b32 passed. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Given an ADV change has no correlated source activity and is draft/non-active, when the dashboard renders, then it appears in `Backlog / inventory`, not `Needs attention`. | pass | Draft/no-source change goes to `backlog`; tr_mqv74wne_95632b32 passed. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | Given an ADV change has correlated source activity, when the dashboard renders, then the change card shows the change title/id/gate plus latest PR, CI, and deployment links/details where present. | pass | ADV change status cards expose title/id/gate plus latest PR/CI/deployment and source details; UI tests tr_mqv7dt3u_d9ea75a9 passed. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | Given a source item cannot be linked to any ADV change, when the dashboard renders, then it appears in a secondary unmatched source section and does not determine primary columns. | pass | Unlinked source remains in secondary `unmatched_source`; model/server tests passed tr_mqv74wne_95632b32/tr_mqv79h3f_d3ed3f73. |
| AC8 | acceptance_criterion | Given GitHub or ADV reads fail, when the dashboard renders, then degraded source cards remain visible and no false green/latest status is inferred. | pass | Degraded sources remain visible and no false green is inferred; model tests and existing degraded server tests passed in tr_mqv7ff29_5df2600b. |
| AC9 | acceptance_criterion | Given source links/metadata render on change-centered cards, when the dashboard loads, then safe URL allowlisting, HTML escaping, no-secret behavior, and no mutation controls remain intact. | pass | UI tests preserve safeUrl, escapeHtml, no forms/mutation controls; tr_mqv7dt3u_d9ea75a9 passed. |
| AC10 | acceptance_criterion | Given dashboard tests run, when `bun test bin/` completes, then latest-run precedence, change-centered columns, unmatched secondary section, degraded fallback, and read-only constraints are covered by tests. | pass | Targeted dashboard tests tr_mqv7ff29_5df2600b passed 20 tests/119 expects; full bin suite tr_mqv7g1mj_6c94a733 passed 165 tests/491 expects. |
| C1 | constraint | Keep dashboard local-only at `127.0.0.1`. | respected | No dashboard host/listener behavior changed; server tests for loopback default passed in tr_mqv79h3f_d3ed3f73 and full tr_mqv7g1mj_6c94a733. |
| C2 | constraint | Read-only UI only: no rerun/merge/approve/deploy/archive/cancel controls. | respected | No mutation controls/forms rendered; UI no-mutation test passed tr_mqv7dt3u_d9ea75a9. |
| C3 | constraint | No token/secret display. | respected | No token/secret display; existing sanitization/degraded tests passed full suite tr_mqv7g1mj_6c94a733. |
| C4 | constraint | GitHub auth model unchanged: `GITHUB_TOKEN` first, then bounded `gh auth token` fallback. | respected | GitHub auth code unchanged; dashboard auth fallback remains GITHUB_TOKEN then gh auth token. |
| C5 | constraint | No fuzzy title-based correctness; source-to-change linking must remain structural (branch/SHA/known keys). | respected | Source linking remains structural in correlation.ts branch/SHA/ops keys; no fuzzy title matching added. |
| C6 | constraint | If latest derivation cannot prove a superseding newer source state, preserve visibility as unknown/unmatched/attention as appropriate rather than hiding risk. | respected | Missing timestamp does not suppress valid failure; degraded/missing data does not false green. tr_mqv74wne_95632b32 passed. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | No GitHub mutation controls. | respected | No GitHub mutation controls added; UI no-mutation tests passed. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | No ADV mutation controls. | respected | No ADV mutation controls added; UI no-mutation tests passed. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | No server-side preference store. | respected | No server-side preference store added. |
| DONT4 | avoidance | No hosted dashboard behavior. | respected | No hosted dashboard behavior added; local server behavior unchanged. |
| DONT5 | avoidance | No false green from missing/ambiguous data. | respected | Missing/ambiguous data does not produce false green; tests cover missing timestamp and degraded source behavior. |
| DONT6 | avoidance | No primary raw event wall. | respected | Primary raw event wall removed; primary cards are ADV change status cards and raw unmatched source is secondary. Tests passed. |

clarify_findings.message: Proposal has no Scope section. What files and modules will be affected?
Proposal references authentication/authorization without specifying the model. What auth mechanism will be used?
Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
